out_of_sample.note: Historical instances MUST be split into discovery (70%) and validation (30%) sets using TEMPORAL splits. Older events = discovery, newer events = validation. This prevents look-ahead bias that random splits allow.
out_of_sample.note2: validate_out_of_sample() auto-splits by date if events have 'date' fields, or use discovery_cutoff_date for explicit control. Index-based splits are deprecated — use only as a last resort.

multiple_testing_correction.note: With 14 categories x 5 horizons = 70 tests, p<0.05 yields ~3.5 false positives. Require significance at 2+ independent horizons OR use Bonferroni-adjusted threshold.
multiple_testing_correction.note2: If only 1 horizon is significant, use bonferroni_adjusted_p (0.01) instead of 0.05. If 2+ horizons significant at 0.05, no correction needed.
multiple_testing_correction.cross_category_fdr_note: Within-backtest correction handles horizons. Across categories (N=14+), apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR using market_data.apply_cross_category_fdr() when reviewing aggregate results.

event_timing.note: Events happen at different times. An after-hours FDA decision moves the stock next morning, not same day. The reference price must account for this.

reference_price_rules.pre_market: Use previous day close as reference. Day-0 close captures the reaction.
reference_price_rules.intraday: Use previous day close as reference. Day-0 close captures partial reaction.
reference_price_rules.after_hours: Use day-0 close as reference (before the event). Day-1 close captures the reaction.
reference_price_rules.unknown: Use previous day close as reference. Note timing uncertainty in hypothesis.

regime_conditioning.note: Effects may be regime-dependent. Always check if the pattern holds across different market environments.
regime_conditioning.requirement: If backtesting has N>=15, subset by regime and report whether effect is regime-dependent. If regime-dependent, note which regime the hypothesis applies to.

focus_rules.note: Research without focus is just burning tokens. These rules prevent scatter.
focus_rules.abandon_note: If 3 consecutive hypotheses on the same signal fail or are abandoned, retire the signal type.
focus_rules.pending_gate: Do not start new signal types until pending queue has < 5 entries.

revalidation.note: No signal is permanent. Markets change. Validated effects must be re-tested on fresh data periodically.
revalidation.on_failure: If revalidation experiment fails, downgrade from VALIDATED to EXPLORING and reset the evidence clock.

experiment_independence.note: Correlated experiments (same signal, same week) count as one independent data point. Promotion/retirement thresholds use effective_independent_n, not raw experiment count.
experiment_independence.clustering: Experiments within window_days trading days of each other are clustered. Each cluster's direction_correct is majority vote.

pattern_states.EXPLORING: Not enough independent data to judge (effective_independent_n < min_live_tests)
pattern_states.PROMISING: Enough data, accuracy above threshold, not yet fully validated
pattern_states.VALIDATED: Meets all promotion criteria (tests, accuracy, magnitude ratio)
pattern_states.RETIRED: Meets retirement criteria (enough tests, accuracy too low)

promotion_criteria.note: When does a live-tested pattern become a 'known effect'? Uses effective_independent_n (not raw experiment count).
promotion_criteria.note2: After min_live_tests independent experiments with accuracy >= min_live_accuracy and avg magnitude_ratio >= min_live_magnitude_ratio, auto-promote to known_effects. After retirement_min_tests with accuracy <= retirement_max_accuracy, auto-retire as dead end.

pre_registration.note: Hypotheses are hashed at creation time. The hash covers: event_type, expected_symbol, expected_direction, expected_magnitude_pct, expected_timeframe_days. This prevents post-hoc adjustment of predictions.
pre_registration.enforcement: Predictions are logged to results.jsonl at creation time, before any trade is placed.

causal_mechanism_rubric.note: A valid causal mechanism must satisfy at least 2 of these 3 criteria. 'Stocks go up because they always do' is not a mechanism.
criteria: Identifies specific economic actors and their incentives (e.g., 'index funds MUST buy added stocks due to tracking mandates')
Explains the transmission channel (e.g., 'FDA rejection removes revenue expectations for the drug, which was X% of projected revenue')
References an established economic principle or academic finding (e.g., 'post-earnings announcement drift is documented in Fama 1998')

survivorship_bias_protocol.note: When backtesting events where companies could have failed (FDA rejections, earnings disasters, bankruptcies), actively search for delisted/failed companies.
survivorship_bias_protocol.requirement: For event types involving potential company failure, include at least 2 delisted/bankrupt companies in the sample, OR explicitly document that no relevant delistings occurred and how you verified this.

data_quality.note: measure_event_impact() can silently drop events with bad data. Track this.
data_quality.requirement: If more than 30% of attempted events fail to produce data, flag the backtest as unreliable and investigate data quality before forming hypotheses.

web_search_protocol.note: Web search is for finding EVENT DATES, not for determining what happened. News articles cherry-pick dramatic examples.
use_for: Finding dates when specific events occurred
web_search_protocol.requirement: After finding event dates via web search, ALWAYS verify the price impact with measure_event_impact(). Never form hypotheses based on news narratives alone.

hypothesis_classes.note: The system supports 10 hypothesis classes. Each has its own test method, data_task command, and validation criteria. Non-event classes use regression-based tests instead of event studies.

event.description: Discrete event -> price reaction in N days

lead_lag.description: Series A leads series B by N days

cointegration.description: Two series share a long-run equilibrium

structural_break.description: Relationship changed at a specific date

cross_section.description: Factor sorts stocks into return-predictive quantiles

confidence_rubric_non_event.note: For non-event hypothesis classes, confidence is computed from regression/test statistics. Score = sum of component scores, capped at 10.

confidence_rubric.note: Confidence is computed from evidence, not assigned by feel. Score = sum of component scores, capped at 10.
confidence_rubric.note2: Components now sum to exactly 10 (3+3+2+2). A hypothesis with N=6, 65% consistency, avg/stdev=0.4, no literature = 1+1+1+0 = 3. Use literature_strength='partial' or 'strong' in compute_confidence_score() to award 1 or 2 points respectively.

confidence_calibration.note: Tracks whether confidence scores predict accuracy. Updated during self-reviews.

methodology_changelog.changes: Initial methodology. All defaults are starting assumptions — no data yet to calibrate against.

Added: min_abnormal_return_pct (1.5%), min_p_value (0.05), confidence rubric (formula-based), survivorship/selection bias tracking, knowledge revalidation decay (12 months), magnitude-aware accuracy in self-review.
methodology_changelog.reason: Methodology audit — statistical rigor and measurability gaps

methodology_changelog.changes: Raised min_sample_size from 5 to 12 (added exploratory tier at 5). Added: out-of-sample split enforcement (70/30), multiple testing correction (require 2+ significant horizons or Bonferroni p<0.01), event timing protocol, regime conditioning, promotion/retirement criteria for live-tested patterns, pre-registration hashing, causal mechanism rubric (2 of 3 criteria), survivorship bias protocol (require delisted companies), data quality checks (max 30% drop rate), web search protocol (dates only — verify with backtest), uniform position sizing (removed P&L optimization loop).
methodology_changelog.reason: Full methodology audit — addressing multiple testing, out-of-sample, event timing, regime conditioning, stopping criteria, pre-registration, and causal mechanism rigor

methodology_changelog.changes: Multi-symbol backtest support (event_dates can now contain {symbol, date} dicts). Temporal out-of-sample splits (older=discovery, newer=validation — index splits deprecated). Replaced hand-rolled p-value with scipy.stats.ttest_1samp. Added power analysis (recommended_n fields in backtest output). Added cross-event contamination detection. Fixed sector-adjusted returns circular reference for large ETF constituents. Added task IDs and deduplication to research queue. Added idempotency keys to prevent duplicate hypotheses on crash+rerun. Added confounder enforcement and analysis in self-review. Added check_related_dead_ends() for negative result utilization. Added weekly research diagnostic. Structured session logs (sessions.jsonl). Raised turn limits from 50 to 80 for operations/research sessions. Split CLAUDE.md into operational reference + METHODOLOGY.md.
methodology_changelog.reason: Infrastructure audit — 16 fixes for research loop reliability, statistical correctness, and cross-session coherence

methodology_changelog.changes: Methodology audit — 13 fixes: (1) cross-category BH FDR utility, (2) Wilcoxon signed-rank robustness check + skewness warnings, (3) confidence rubric SNR component reduced to max 2 (components sum to 10), (4) placeholder evidence detection blocks fake OOS validation, (5) min_abnormal_return_pct enforced in create_hypothesis, (6) direction correctness requires min 0.5% threshold, (7) confounder validation now blocking, (8) backtest_events type documented, (9) stale hypothesis detection in weekly diagnostic, (10) magnitude ratio direction-aware for shorts, (11) transaction cost model (0.1% round-trip, 1.0% min net), (12) regime_filter param for VIX-conditional backtesting, (13) prediction hash tamper check at completion.
methodology_changelog.reason: Methodology audit — statistical robustness, validation integrity, and missing enforcement

methodology_changelog.changes: Focus and discipline overhaul: (1) MAX_ACTIVE_SIGNAL_TYPES=3 — block new signal types when at capacity, (2) MAX_CONCURRENT_PER_SIGNAL=2 — correlated positions are not independent data, (3) experiment independence counting — same signal within 5 trading days clusters as 1 data point, (4) pattern lifecycle state machine (EXPLORING/PROMISING/FAILING/VALIDATED/RETIRED), (5) auto-promotion and auto-retirement when thresholds met, (6) signal revalidation every 6 months — no signal is permanent, (7) check_factors and check_seasonal now default to True in measure_event_impact, (8) honest labeling — known_effects renamed to 'research findings (backtest)' in reports, (9) daily email shows pattern state, effective independent N, and what each signal needs next, (10) focus discipline added to agent constitution.
methodology_changelog.reason: System audit revealed 22 scattered signal types, 4% completion rate, and $690 in tokens for $50 P&L. Research needs focus, not more features.
